NKJV.1: Now on the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they, and certain other women with them, came to the tomb bringing the spices which they had prepared.
NKJV.2: But they found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
NKJV.3: Then they went in and did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
NKJV.4: And it happened, as they were greatly perplexed about this, that behold, two men stood by them in shining garments.
NKJV.5: Then, as they were afraid and bowed their faces to the earth, they said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?
NKJV.6: He is not here, but is risen! Remember how He spoke to you when He was still in Galilee,
NKJV.7: saying, ‘The Son of Man must be  delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.’ ”
NKJV.9: Then they returned from the tomb and told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
NKJV.10: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them, who told these things to the apostles.
NKJV.11: And their words seemed to them like idle tales, and they did not believe them.
NKJV.12: But Peter arose and ran to the tomb; and stooping down, he saw the linen cloths lying by themselves; and he departed, marveling to himself at what had happened.
NKJV.13: Now behold, two of them were traveling that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was seven miles from Jerusalem.
NKJV.14: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
NKJV.15: So it was, while they conversed and reasoned, that Jesus Himself drew near and went with them.
NKJV.16: But their eyes were restrained, so that they did not know Him.
NKJV.17: And He said to them, “What kind of conversation is this that you have with one another as you walk and are sad?”
NKJV.18: Then the one whose name was Cleopas answered and said to Him, “Are You the only stranger in Jerusalem, and have You not known the things which happened there in these days?”
NKJV.19: And He said to them, “What things?” So they said to Him, “The things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a Prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
NKJV.20: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered Him to be condemned to death, and crucified Him.
NKJV.21: But we were hoping that it was He who was going to redeem Israel. Indeed, besides all this, today is the third day since these things happened.
NKJV.22: Yes, and certain women of our company, who arrived at the tomb early, astonished us.
NKJV.23: When they did not find His body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels who said He was alive.
NKJV.24: And certain of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said; but Him they did not see.”
NKJV.25: Then He said to them, “O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!
NKJV.26: Ought not the Christ to have suffered these things and to enter into His  glory?”
NKJV.27: And beginning at Moses and all the Prophets, He expounded to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning Himself.
NKJV.28: Then they drew near to the village where they were going, and He indicated that He would have gone farther.
NKJV.29: But they constrained Him, saying, “Abide with us, for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent.” And He went in to stay with them.
NKJV.30: Now it came to pass, as He sat at the table with them, that He took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
NKJV.31: Then their eyes were opened and they knew Him; and He vanished from their sight.
NKJV.32: And they said to one another, “Did not our heart burn within us while He talked with us on the road, and while He opened the Scriptures to us?”
NKJV.33: So they rose up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven and those who were with them gathered together,
NKJV.34: saying, “The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”
NKJV.35: And they told about the things that had happened on the road, and how He was known to them in the breaking of bread.
NKJV.36: Now as they said these things, Jesus Himself stood in the midst of them, and said to them, “Peace to you.”
NKJV.37: But they were terrified and frightened, and supposed they had seen a spirit.
NKJV.38: And He said to them, “Why are you troubled? And why do doubts arise in your hearts?
NKJV.39: Behold My hands and My feet, that it is I Myself.  Handle Me and see, for a  spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
NKJV.40: When He had said this, He showed them His hands and His feet.
NKJV.41: But while they still did not believe for joy, and marveled, He said to them, “Have you any food here?”
NKJV.42: So they gave Him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.
NKJV.43: And He took it and ate in their presence.
NKJV.44: Then He said to them, “These are the words which I spoke to you while I was still with you, that all things must be fulfilled which were written in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms concerning Me.”
NKJV.45: And He opened their understanding, that they might comprehend the Scriptures.
NKJV.46: Then He said to them, “Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,
NKJV.47: and that repentance and  remission of sins should be preached in His name  to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
NKJV.48: And  you are witnesses of these things.
NKJV.49: Behold, I send the Promise of My Father upon you; but tarry in the city of Jerusalem until you are endued with power from on high.”
NKJV.50: And He led them out as far as Bethany, and He lifted up His hands and blessed them.
NKJV.51: Now it came to pass, while He blessed them, that He was parted from them and carried up into heaven.
NKJV.52: And they worshiped Him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
NKJV.53: and were continually in the temple praising and blessing God. Amen.

ESV.1: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they went to the tomb, taking the spices they had prepared.
ESV.2: And they found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
ESV.3: but when they went in they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
ESV.4: While they were perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling apparel.
ESV.5: And as they were frightened and bowed their faces to the ground, the men said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?
ESV.6: He is not here, but has risen. Remember how he told you, while he was still in Galilee,
ESV.7: that the Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men and be crucified and on the third day rise.”
ESV.9: and returning from the tomb they told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
ESV.10: Now it was Mary Magdalene and Joanna and Mary the mother of James and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles,
ESV.11: but these words seemed to them an idle tale, and they did not believe them.
ESV.12: But Peter rose and ran to the tomb; stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths by themselves; and he went home marveling at what had happened.
ESV.13: That very day two of them were going to a village named Emmaus, about seven miles  from Jerusalem,
ESV.14: and they were talking with each other about all these things that had happened.
ESV.15: While they were talking and discussing together, Jesus himself drew near and went with them.
ESV.16: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.
ESV.17: And he said to them, “What is this conversation that you are holding with each other as you walk?” And they stood still, looking sad.
ESV.18: Then one of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in these days?”
ESV.19: And he said to them, “What things?” And they said to him, “Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, a man who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people,
ESV.20: and how our chief priests and rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.
ESV.21: But we had hoped that he was the one to redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.
ESV.22: Moreover, some women of our company amazed us. They were at the tomb early in the morning,
ESV.23: and when they did not find his body, they came back saying that they had even seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.
ESV.24: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see.”
ESV.25: And he said to them, “O foolish ones, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
ESV.26: Was it not necessary that the Christ should suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
ESV.27: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.
ESV.28: So they drew near to the village to which they were going. He acted as if he were going farther,
ESV.29: but they urged him strongly, saying, “Stay with us, for it is toward evening and the day is now far spent.” So he went in to stay with them.
ESV.30: When he was at table with them, he took the bread and blessed and broke it and gave it to them.
ESV.31: And their eyes were opened, and they recognized him. And he vanished from their sight.
ESV.32: They said to each other, “Did not our hearts burn within us while he talked to us on the road, while he opened to us the Scriptures?”
ESV.33: And they rose that same hour and returned to Jerusalem. And they found the eleven and those who were with them gathered together,
ESV.34: saying, “The Lord has risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”
ESV.35: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how he was known to them in the breaking of the bread.
ESV.36: As they were talking about these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace to you!”
ESV.37: But they were startled and frightened and thought they saw a spirit.
ESV.38: And he said to them, “Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
ESV.39: See my hands and my feet, that it is I myself. Touch me, and see. For a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see that I have.”
ESV.40: And when he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
ESV.41: And while they still disbelieved for joy and were marveling, he said to them, “Have you anything here to eat?”
ESV.42: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
ESV.43: and he took it and ate before them.
ESV.44: Then he said to them,  “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the Law of Moses and the Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled.”
ESV.45: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures,
ESV.46: and said to them, “Thus it is written, that the Christ should suffer and on the third day rise from the dead,
ESV.47: and that repentance for  the forgiveness of sins should be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
ESV.48: You are witnesses of these things.
ESV.49: And behold, I am sending the promise of my Father upon you. But stay in the city until you are clothed with power from on high.”
ESV.50: And he led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them.
ESV.51: While he blessed them, he parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
ESV.52: And they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
ESV.53: and were continually in the temple blessing God.

NLT.1: But very early on Sunday morning the women went to the tomb, taking the spices they had prepared.
NLT.2: They found that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
NLT.3: So they went in, but they didn’t find the body of the Lord Jesus.
NLT.4: As they stood there puzzled, two men suddenly appeared to them, clothed in dazzling robes.
NLT.5: The women were terrified and bowed with their faces to the ground. Then the men asked, “Why are you looking among the dead for someone who is alive?
NLT.6: He isn’t here! He is risen from the dead! Remember what he told you back in Galilee,
NLT.7: that the Son of Man must be betrayed into the hands of sinful men and be crucified, and that he would rise again on the third day.”
NLT.8: Then they remembered that he had said this.
NLT.9: So they rushed back from the tomb to tell his eleven disciples — and everyone else — what had happened.
NLT.10: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and several other women who told the apostles what had happened.
NLT.11: But the story sounded like nonsense to the men, so they didn’t believe it.
NLT.12: However, Peter jumped up and ran to the tomb to look. Stooping, he peered in and saw the empty linen wrappings; then he went home again, wondering what had happened.
NLT.13: That same day two of Jesus’ followers were walking to the village of Emmaus, seven miles from Jerusalem.
NLT.14: As they walked along they were talking about everything that had happened.
NLT.15: As they talked and discussed these things, Jesus himself suddenly came and began walking with them.
NLT.16: But God kept them from recognizing him.
NLT.17: He asked them, “What are you discussing so intently as you walk along?” They stopped short, sadness written across their faces.
NLT.18: Then one of them, Cleopas, replied, “You must be the only person in Jerusalem who hasn’t heard about all the things that have happened there the last few days.”
NLT.19: “What things?” Jesus asked. “The things that happened to Jesus, the man from Nazareth,” they said. “He was a prophet who did powerful miracles, and he was a mighty teacher in the eyes of God and all the people.
NLT.20: But our leading priests and other religious leaders handed him over to be condemned to death, and they crucified him.
NLT.21: We had hoped he was the Messiah who had come to rescue Israel. This all happened three days ago.
NLT.22: “Then some women from our group of his followers were at his tomb early this morning, and they came back with an amazing report.
NLT.23: They said his body was missing, and they had seen angels who told them Jesus is alive!
NLT.24: Some of our men ran out to see, and sure enough, his body was gone, just as the women had said.”
NLT.25: Then Jesus said to them, “You foolish people! You find it so hard to believe all that the prophets wrote in the Scriptures.
NLT.26: Wasn’t it clearly predicted that the Messiah would have to suffer all these things before entering his glory?”
NLT.27: Then Jesus took them through the writings of Moses and all the prophets, explaining from all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.
NLT.28: By this time they were nearing Emmaus and the end of their journey. Jesus acted as if he were going on,
NLT.29: but they begged him, “Stay the night with us, since it is getting late.” So he went home with them.
NLT.30: As they sat down to eat, he took the bread and blessed it. Then he broke it and gave it to them.
NLT.31: Suddenly, their eyes were opened, and they recognized him. And at that moment he disappeared!
NLT.32: They said to each other, “Didn’t our hearts burn within us as he talked with us on the road and explained the Scriptures to us?”
NLT.33: And within the hour they were on their way back to Jerusalem. There they found the eleven disciples and the others who had gathered with them,
NLT.34: who said, “The Lord has really risen! He appeared to Peter. ”
NLT.35: Then the two from Emmaus told their story of how Jesus had appeared to them as they were walking along the road, and how they had recognized him as he was breaking the bread.
NLT.36: And just as they were telling about it, Jesus himself was suddenly standing there among them. “Peace be with you,” he said.
NLT.37: But the whole group was startled and frightened, thinking they were seeing a ghost!
NLT.38: “Why are you frightened?” he asked. “Why are your hearts filled with doubt?
NLT.39: Look at my hands. Look at my feet. You can see that it’s really me. Touch me and make sure that I am not a ghost, because ghosts don’t have bodies, as you see that I do.”
NLT.40: As he spoke, he showed them his hands and his feet.
NLT.41: Still they stood there in disbelief, filled with joy and wonder. Then he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
NLT.42: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
NLT.43: and he ate it as they watched.
NLT.44: Then he said, “When I was with you before, I told you that everything written about me in the law of Moses and the prophets and in the Psalms must be fulfilled.”
NLT.45: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.
NLT.46: And he said, “Yes, it was written long ago that the Messiah would suffer and die and rise from the dead on the third day.
NLT.47: It was also written that this message would be proclaimed in the authority of his name to all the nations, beginning in Jerusalem: ‘There is forgiveness of sins for all who repent.’
NLT.48: You are witnesses of all these things.
NLT.49: “And now I will send the Holy Spirit, just as my Father promised. But stay here in the city until the Holy Spirit comes and fills you with power from heaven.”
NLT.50: Then Jesus led them to Bethany, and lifting his hands to heaven, he blessed them.
NLT.51: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
NLT.52: So they worshiped him and then returned to Jerusalem filled with great joy.
NLT.53: And they spent all of their time in the Temple, praising God.

CSB17.24: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but they didn’t see him.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.25: He said to them, “How foolish and slow <sup>[6]</sup>you are to believe all that the prophets have spoken! <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.26: Wasn’t it necessary for the Messiah to suffer <sup>ⓖ</sup>these things and enter into his glory?” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.27: Then beginning with Moses <sup>ⓘ</sup> and all the Prophets, <sup>ⓙ</sup> he interpreted for them the things concerning himself in all the Scriptures. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.28: They came near the village where they were going, and he gave the impression that he was going farther. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.29: But they urged him, “Stay with us, because it’s almost evening, and now the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.
CSB17.30: It was as he reclined at the table with them that he took the bread, blessed and broke <sup>ⓜ</sup> it, and gave it to them.
CSB17.31: Then their eyes were opened, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.32: They said to each other, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us while he was talking with us on the road and explaining the Scriptures <sup>ⓑ</sup> to us?”
CSB17.33: That very hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem. They found the Eleven <sup>ⓒ</sup> and those with them gathered together,
CSB17.34: who said, “The Lord has truly been raised and has appeared to Simon!” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.35: Then they began to describe what had happened on the road and how he was made known to them in the breaking of the bread. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.36: As <sup>ⓕ</sup> they were saying these things, he himself stood in their midst. He said to them, “Peace <sup>ⓖ</sup>to you!”
CSB17.37: But they were startled and terrified <sup>ⓗ</sup> and thought they were seeing a ghost. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.38: “Why are you troubled?” <sup>ⓙ</sup> he asked them. “And why do doubts arise in your hearts?
CSB17.39: Look at my hands and my feet, that it is I myself! <sup>ⓚ</sup>Touch me and see, <sup>ⓛ</sup>because a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you can see I have.”
CSB17.40: Having said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
CSB17.41: But while they still were amazed and in disbelief because of their joy, he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
CSB17.42: So they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, <sup>[7]</sup>
CSB17.43: and he took it and ate in their presence.
CSB17.44: He told them, “These are my words <sup>ⓜ</sup> that I spoke to you while I was still with you — that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and the Psalms <sup>ⓐ</sup> must be fulfilled.”
CSB17.45: Then he opened their minds <sup>ⓑ</sup> to understand <sup>ⓒ</sup> the Scriptures.
CSB17.46: He also said to them, “This is what is written: <sup>[8]</sup>The Messiah would suffer and rise from the dead the third day, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.47: and repentance for <sup>[9]</sup> forgiveness of sins <sup>ⓔ</sup>would be proclaimed in his name to all the nations, <sup>ⓕ</sup>beginning at Jerusalem. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.49: And look, I am sending you <sup>[10]</sup>what my Father promised. <sup>ⓘ</sup>As for you, stay in the city <sup>[11]</sup>until you are empowered <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>from on high.”
CSB17.50: Then he led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and lifting up his hands <sup>ⓛ</sup> he blessed them.
CSB17.51: And while he was blessing them, he left them and was carried up into heaven. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.52: After worshiping him, <sup>ⓝ</sup> they returned to Jerusalem with great joy. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.53: And they were continually in the temple praising God. <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>

AMP.1: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, the women went to the tomb bringing the spices which they had prepared [to finish anointing the body].
AMP.2: And they found the [large, circular] stone rolled back from the tomb,
AMP.3: but when they went inside, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
AMP.4: While they were perplexed and wondering about this, suddenly, two men in dazzling clothing stood near them;
AMP.5: and as the women were terrified and were bowing their faces to the ground, the men said to them, "Why are you looking for the living One among the dead?
AMP.6: He is not here, but has risen. Remember how He told you, while He was still in Galilee,
AMP.7: saying that the Son of Man must be handed over to sinful men, and be crucified, and on the third day rise [from death to life]."
AMP.9: and after returning from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven [apostles] and to all the rest.
AMP.10: Now they were Mary Magdalene and Joanna [the wife of Chuza, Herod's steward] and Mary the mother of James; also the other women with them were telling these things to the apostles.
AMP.11: But their report seemed to them like idle talk and nonsense, and they would not believe them.
AMP.12: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping [at the small entrance] and looking in, he saw only the linen wrappings; and he went away, wondering about what had happened.
AMP.13: And then, that very day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, which was about seven miles from Jerusalem.
AMP.14: And they were talking with each other about all these things which had taken place.
AMP.15: While they were talking and discussing it, Jesus Himself came up and began walking with them.
AMP.16: But their eyes were [miraculously] prevented from recognizing Him.
AMP.17: Then Jesus asked them, "What are you discussing with one another as you walk along?" And they stood still, looking brokenhearted.
AMP.18: One of them, named Cleopas, answered Him, "Are you the only stranger visiting Jerusalem who is unaware of the things which have happened here in these [recent] days?"
AMP.19: He asked, "What things?" And they replied, "The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet powerful in deed and word in the sight of God and all the people,
AMP.20: and how the chief priests and our rulers handed Him over to be sentenced to death, and crucified Him.
AMP.21: But we were hoping that it was He who was going to redeem Israel and set our nation free. Indeed, besides all this, it is the third day since these things happened.
AMP.22: And also some of the women among us shocked us. They were at the tomb early in the morning,
AMP.23: and they did not find His body. Then they came back, saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who said that He was alive!
AMP.24: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just exactly as the women had said, but they did not see Him."
AMP.25: Then Jesus said to them, "O foolish men, and slow of heart to trust and believe in everything that the prophets have spoken!
AMP.26: Was it not necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and [only then to] enter His glory?"
AMP.27: Then beginning with Moses and [throughout] all the [writings of the] prophets, He explained and interpreted for them the things referring to Himself [found] in all the Scriptures.
AMP.28: Then they approached the village where they were going, and He acted as if He were going farther.
AMP.29: But they urged Him [not to go on], saying, "Stay with us, because it is almost evening, and the day has just about ended." So He went inside to stay with them.
AMP.30: And it happened that as He reclined at the table with them, He took the bread and blessed it, and breaking it, He began giving it to them.
AMP.31: Then their eyes were [suddenly] opened [by God] and they [clearly] recognized Him; and He vanished from their sight.
AMP.32: They said to one another, "Were not our hearts burning within us while He was talking with us on the road and opening the Scriptures to us?"
AMP.33: They got up that very hour and went back to Jerusalem, and found the eleven [apostles] gathered together and those who were with them,
AMP.34: saying, "The Lord has really risen and has appeared to Simon [Peter]!"
AMP.35: They began describing in detail what had happened on the road, and how Jesus was recognized by them when He broke the bread.
AMP.36: While they were talking about this, Jesus Himself [suddenly] stood among them and said to them, "Peace be to you."
AMP.37: But they were startled and terrified and thought that they were seeing a spirit.
AMP.38: And He said, "Why are you troubled, and why are doubts rising in your hearts?
AMP.39: Look at [the marks in] My hands and My feet, [and see] that it is I Myself. Touch Me and see; a spirit does not have flesh and bones, as you see that I have."
AMP.40: After saying this, He showed them His hands and His feet.
AMP.41: While they still did not believe it because of their joy and amazement, He asked them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"
AMP.42: They gave Him a piece of broiled fish,
AMP.43: and He took it and ate it in front of them.
AMP.44: Then He said to them, "This is what I told you while I was still with you, everything which has been written about Me in the Law of Moses and the [writings of the] Prophets and the Psalms must be fulfilled."
AMP.45: Then He opened their minds to [help them] understand the Scriptures,
AMP.46: and said, "And so it is written, that the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) would suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
AMP.47: and that repentance [necessary] for forgiveness of sins would be preached in His name to all the nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
AMP.48: You are witnesses of these things.
AMP.49: Listen carefully: I am sending the Promise of My Father [the Holy Spirit] upon you; but you are to remain in the city [of Jerusalem] until you are clothed (fully equipped) with power from on high."
AMP.50: Then He led them out as far as Bethany, and lifted up His hands and blessed them.
AMP.51: While He was blessing them, He left them and was taken up into heaven.
AMP.52: And they worshiped Him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy [fully understanding that He lives and that He is the Son of God];
AMP.53: and they were continually in the temple blessing and praising God.

MSG.1: At the crack of dawn on Sunday, the women came to the tomb carrying the burial spices they had prepared.
MSG.2: They found the entrance stone rolled back from the tomb,
MSG.3: so they walked in. But once inside, they couldn't find the body of the Master Jesus.
MSG.4: They were puzzled, wondering what to make of this. Then, out of nowhere it seemed, two men, light cascading over them, stood there.
MSG.5: The women were awestruck and bowed down in worship. The men said, "Why are you looking for the Living One in a cemetery?
MSG.6: He is not here, but raised up. Remember how he told you when you were still back in Galilee
MSG.7: that he had to be handed over to sinners, be killed on a cross, and in three days rise up?"
MSG.9: They left the tomb and broke the news of all this to the Eleven and the rest.
MSG.10: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them kept telling these things to the apostles,
MSG.11: but the apostles didn't believe a word of it, thought they were making it all up.
MSG.12: But Peter jumped to his feet and ran to the tomb. He stooped to look in and saw a few grave clothes, that's all. He walked away puzzled, shaking his head.
MSG.13: That same day two of them were walking to the village Emmaus, about seven miles out of Jerusalem.
MSG.14: They were deep in conversation, going over all these things that had happened.
MSG.15: In the middle of their talk and questions, Jesus came up and walked along with them.
MSG.16: But they were not able to recognize who he was.
MSG.17: He asked, "What's this you're discussing so intently as you walk along?" They just stood there, long-faced, like they had lost their best friend.
MSG.18: Then one of them, his name was Cleopas, said, "Are you the only one in Jerusalem who hasn't heard what's happened during the last few days?"
MSG.19: He said, "What has happened?" They said, "The things that happened to Jesus the Nazarene. He was a man of God, a prophet, dynamic in work and word, blessed by both God and all the people.
MSG.20: Then our high priests and leaders betrayed him, got him sentenced to death, and crucified him.
MSG.21: And we had our hopes up that he was the One, the One about to deliver Israel. And it is now the third day since it happened.
MSG.22: But now some of our women have completely confused us. Early this morning they were at the tomb
MSG.23: and couldn't find his body. They came back with the story that they had seen a vision of angels who said he was alive.
MSG.24: Some of our friends went off to the tomb to check and found it empty just as the women said, but they didn't see Jesus."
MSG.25: Then he said to them, "So thick-headed! So slow-hearted! Why can't you simply believe all that the prophets said?
MSG.26: Don't you see that these things had to happen, that the Messiah had to suffer and only then enter into his glory?"
MSG.27: Then he started at the beginning, with the Books of Moses, and went on through all the Prophets, pointing out everything in the Scriptures that referred to him.
MSG.28: They came to the edge of the village where they were headed. He acted as if he were going on
MSG.29: but they pressed him: "Stay and have supper with us. It's nearly evening; the day is done." So he went in with them.
MSG.30: And here is what happened: He sat down at the table with them. Taking the bread, he blessed and broke and gave it to them.
MSG.31: At that moment, open-eyed, wide-eyed, they recognized him. And then he disappeared.
MSG.32: Back and forth they talked. "Didn't we feel on fire as he conversed with us on the road, as he opened up the Scriptures for us?"
MSG.33: They didn't waste a minute. They were up and on their way back to Jerusalem. They found the Eleven and their friends gathered together,
MSG.34: talking away: "It's really happened! The Master has been raised up—Simon saw him!"
MSG.35: Then the two went over everything that happened on the road and how they recognized him when he broke the bread.
MSG.36: While they were saying all this, Jesus appeared to them and said, "Peace be with you."
MSG.37: They thought they were seeing a ghost and were scared half to death.
MSG.38: He continued with them, "Don't be upset, and don't let all these doubting questions take over.
MSG.39: Look at my hands; look at my feet—it's really me. Touch me. Look me over from head to toe. A ghost doesn't have muscle and bone like this."
MSG.40: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
MSG.41: They still couldn't believe what they were seeing. It was too much; it seemed too good to be true. He asked, "Do you have any food here?"
MSG.42: They gave him a piece of leftover fish they had cooked.
MSG.43: He took it and ate it right before their eyes.
MSG.44: Then he said, "Everything I told you while I was with you comes to this: All the things written about me in the Law of Moses, in the Prophets, and in the Psalms have to be fulfilled."
MSG.45: He went on to open their understanding of the Word of God, showing them how to read their Bibles this way.
MSG.46: He said, "You can see now how it is written that the Messiah suffers, rises from the dead on the third day,
MSG.47: and then a total life-change through the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed in his name to all nations—starting from here, from Jerusalem!
MSG.48: You're the first to hear and see it. You're the witnesses.
MSG.49: What comes next is very important: I am sending what my Father promised to you, so stay here in the city until he arrives, until you're equipped with power from on high."
MSG.50: He then led them out of the city over to Bethany. Raising his hands he blessed them,
MSG.51: and while blessing them, took his leave, being carried up to heaven.
MSG.52: And they were on their knees, worshiping him. They returned to Jerusalem bursting with joy.
MSG.53: They spent all their time in the Temple praising God. Yes.

NIV.1:
The Resurrection
On the first day of the week, very early in the morning, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb.
NIV.2: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb,
NIV.3: but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
NIV.4: While they were wondering about this, suddenly two men in clothes that gleamed like lightning stood beside them.
NIV.5: In their fright the women bowed down with their faces to the ground, but the men said to them, “Why do you look for the living among the dead?
NIV.6: He is not here; he has risen! Remember how he told you, while he was still with you in Galilee:
NIV.7: ‘The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, be crucified and on the third day be raised again.’ ”
NIV.9: When they came back from the tomb, they told all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
NIV.10: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the others with them who told this to the apostles.
NIV.11: But they did not believe the women, because their words seemed to them like nonsense.
NIV.12: Peter, however, got up and ran to the tomb. Bending over, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he went away, wondering to himself what had happened.
NIV.13:
On the Road to Emmaus
Now that same day two of them were going to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
NIV.14: They were talking with each other about everything that had happened.
NIV.15: As they talked and discussed these things with each other, Jesus himself came up and walked along with them;
NIV.16: but they were kept from recognizing him.
NIV.17:
He asked them, “What are you discussing together as you walk along?”
They stood still, their faces downcast.
NIV.18: One of them, named Cleopas, asked him, “Are you only a visitor to Jerusalem and do not know the things that have happened there in these days?”
NIV.19:
“What things?” he asked.
“About Jesus of Nazareth,” they replied. “He was a prophet, powerful in word and deed before God and all the people.
NIV.20: The chief priests and our rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him;
NIV.21: but we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. And what is more, it is the third day since all this took place.
NIV.22: In addition, some of our women amazed us. They went to the tomb early this morning
NIV.23: but didn’t find his body. They came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
NIV.24: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see.”
NIV.25: He said to them, “How foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
NIV.26: Did not the Christ have to suffer these things and then enter his glory?”
NIV.27: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.
NIV.28: As they approached the village to which they were going, Jesus acted as if he were going farther.
NIV.29: But they urged him strongly, “Stay with us, for it is nearly evening; the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.
NIV.30: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, gave thanks, broke it and began to give it to them.
NIV.31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, and he disappeared from their sight.
NIV.32: They asked each other, “Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?”
NIV.33: They got up and returned at once to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, assembled together
NIV.34: and saying, “It is true! The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon.”
NIV.35: Then the two told what had happened on the way, and how Jesus was recognized by them when he broke the bread.
NIV.36:
Jesus Appears to the Disciples
While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
NIV.37: They were startled and frightened, thinking they saw a ghost.
NIV.38: He said to them, “Why are you troubled, and why do doubts rise in your minds?
NIV.39: Look at my hands and my feet. It is I myself! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have.”
NIV.40: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
NIV.41: And while they still did not believe it because of joy and amazement, he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
NIV.42: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
NIV.43: and he took it and ate it in their presence.
NIV.44: He said to them, “This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms.”
NIV.45: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.
NIV.46: He told them, “This is what is written: The Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
NIV.47: and repentance and forgiveness of sins will be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
NIV.48: You are witnesses of these things.
NIV.49: I am going to send you what my Father has promised; but stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”
NIV.50:
The Ascension
When he had led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, he lifted up his hands and blessed them.
NIV.51: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up into heaven.
NIV.52: Then they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
NIV.53: And they stayed continually at the temple, praising God.

NET.11: But these words seemed like pure nonsense to them, and they did not believe them.
NET.12: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. He bent down and saw only the strips of linen cloth; then he went home, wondering what had happened.
NET.13: Now that very day two of them were on their way to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
NET.14: They were talking to each other about all the things that had happened.
NET.15: While they were talking and debating these things, Jesus himself approached and began to accompany them
NET.16: (but their eyes were kept from recognizing him).
NET.17: Then he said to them, “What are these matters you are discussing so intently as you walk along?” And they stood still, looking sad.
NET.18: Then one of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things that have happened there in these days?”
NET.19: He said to them, “What things?” “The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene,” they replied, “a man who, with his powerful deeds and words, proved to be a prophet before God and all the people;
NET.20: and how our chief priests and rulers handed him over to be condemned to death, and crucified him.
NET.21: But we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. Not only this, but it is now the third day since these things happened.
NET.22: Furthermore, some women of our group amazed us. They were at the tomb early this morning,
NET.23: and when they did not find his body, they came back and said they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
NET.24: Then some of those who were with us went to the tomb, and found it just as the women had said, but they did not see him.”
NET.25: So he said to them, “You foolish people — how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
NET.26: Wasn’t it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and enter into his glory?”
NET.27: Then beginning with Moses and all the prophets, he interpreted to them the things written about himself in all the scriptures.
NET.28: So they approached the village where they were going. He acted as though he wanted to go farther,
NET.29: but they urged him, “Stay with us, because it is getting toward evening and the day is almost done.” So he went in to stay with them.
NET.30: When he had taken his place at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
NET.31: At this point their eyes were opened and they recognized him. Then he vanished out of their sight.
NET.32: They said to each other, “Didn’t our hearts burn within us while he was speaking with us on the road, while he was explaining the scriptures to us?”
NET.33: So they got up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and those with them gathered together
NET.34: and saying, “The Lord has really risen, and has appeared to Simon!”
NET.35: Then they told what had happened on the road, and how they recognized him when he broke the bread.
NET.36: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
NET.37: But they were startled and terrified, thinking they saw a ghost.
NET.38: Then he said to them, “Why are you frightened, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
NET.39: Look at my hands and my feet; it’s me! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones like you see I have.”
NET.40: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
NET.41: And while they still could not believe it (because of their joy) and were amazed, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
NET.42: So they gave him a piece of broiled fish,
NET.43: and he took it and ate it in front of them.
NET.44: Then he said to them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you, that everything written about me in the law of Moses and the prophets and the psalms must be fulfilled.”
NET.45: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the scriptures,
NET.46: and said to them, “Thus it stands written that the Christ would suffer and would rise from the dead on the third day,
NET.47: and repentance for the forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
NET.48: You are witnesses of these things.
NET.49: And look, I am sending you what my Father promised. But stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high.”
NET.50: Then Jesus led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands, he blessed them.
NET.51: Now during the blessing he departed and was taken up into heaven.
NET.52: So they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,
NET.53: and were continually in the temple courts blessing God.
